Florida Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
No Interstate Compact

How to Transfer Your Dietitian License to Florida

There is no active interstate compact covering dietitian practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Council.

Endorsement fee
$165
Processing time
30 days (initial review)
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Registration and Education

    Complete your degree and supervised practice. Most applicants use their active CDR registration as proof of passing a recognized examination.

  2. Apply Online

    Submit your application for Licensure by Endorsement or Examination through the Florida Department of Health MQA Services portal and pay the $165 total fee.

  3. Background Screening

    Undergo fingerprinting for a criminal history background check through a Livescan service provider.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$165
Processing time
30 days (initial review)
Renewal fee
$95
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years + 30 CE hours

Required Documents

  • Official transcripts: Must be sent directly from the educational institution.
  • Proof of Examination or CDR: Verification sent directly to the Board.

Board Contact

Board
Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Council
Department
Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine
Phone
(850) 245-4373
Email
mqa.dieteticsnutrition@flhealth.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $95. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years + 30 CE hours.

Florida Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Florida dietitian license cost?

The Florida Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Council charges a total initial fee of $165. This includes a $50 application fee, $110 license fee, and $5 unlicensed activity fee.

Is a background check required for a Florida dietitian license?

Yes. All applicants must undergo a Livescan fingerprint background check as part of the application process with the Florida Department of Health.

Does Florida have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Florida?

Most endorsement applications take 30 days (initial review). If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
